Zero Hunger.reason: A dedicated topic 'How Food and Other Things Built the World' relates to food as a commodity in global trade and markets (Targets 2.b, 2.c).

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and 'humanities and social science training' for future careers, aligning with skills for employment (Target 4.4).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Core topics like 'International Business,' 'Entrepreneurship,' and 'Finance' directly address economic growth, productivity, and job creation policies (Targets 8.1, 8.2, 8.3).
evidence: Finance, Accounting and International Political Economy

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Focuses on the 'influence of technology,' 'Semiconductors, Chip and Geopolitics,' and 'AI and Metaverse,' directly linking to innovation and technological capabilities (Targets 9.5, 9.b).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about 'international relations,' 'international security,' and 'Geopolitics,' directly addressing the development of effective global institutions and governance (Targets 16.6, 16.8).
